elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Investigate and describe the characteristics and physical properties of objects and solid or nonsolid materials (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture, smell, and sound).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Characteristics of Objects and Materials ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Investigate and describe the characteristics and physical properties of objects and solid or nonsolid materials

elements.title: Characteristics of Objects and Materials
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Investigate and describe in greater detail the characteristics and physical properties of objects and solid, liquid, or gas materials (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture, smell, and sound).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Characteristics of Objects and Materials ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Investigate and describe in greater detail the characteristics and physical properties of objects

elements.title: Light and Sound Waves
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Notice and explore sound, light, and shadows by using their senses and by manipulating different objects and materials during play and collaborative investigations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Light and Sound Waves ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Notice and explore sound, light, and shadows

elements.title: Light and Sound Waves
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Explore and describe changes in the properties of sound, light, and shadows by manipulating objects and materials during play and collaborative investigations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Light and Sound Waves ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Explore and describe changes in the properties of sound, light, and shadows

elements.title: Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Explore and describe changes in objects and materials using their senses (for example, change in color, shape, texture, temperature) during play and collaborative investigations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Explore and describe changes in objects and materials using their senses

elements.title: Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Explore, describe in greater detail, and explain changes in objects and materials using their senses (for example, change in color, shape, texture, form, temperature) during play and collaborative investigations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Exploring Changes in Objects and Materials ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Explore, describe in greater detail, and explain changes

elements.title: Force and Motion
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Observe and describe the way objects' speed and direction change and explore the effect of their own actions (for example, pushing, pulling, rolling, dropping) on making objects move or stop during play and collaborative investigations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Force and Motion ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Observe and describe the way objects' speed and direction change

elements.title: Force and Motion
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Make and test predictions about how objects change direction, speed, or the distance they go and, based on their observations, explain why objects start, stop, or change direction or speed during play and collaborative investigations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Force and Motion ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Make and test predictions about how objects change direction

elements.title: Energy
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, that things (living and nonliving) need sources of energy to function.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Energy ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, that things (living and nonliving) need sources of energy

elements.title: Energy
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, of the different sources of energy that things (living and nonliving) need and describe the changes they observe as a result of these sources of energy.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Energy ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Demonstrate awareness, with adult support, of the different sources of energy

elements.title: Life Science
elements.source_text: Strand: 3.0 — Life Science

elements.title: Properties and Characteristics of Living Things
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Properties and Characteristics of Living Things

elements.title: Characteristics of Living Things
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Identify and describe characteristics of a variety of animals and plants, including appearance (inside and outside) and behavior, and demonstrate an emerging ability to categorize them.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Characteristics of Living Things ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Identify and describe characteristics of a variety of animals and plants

elements.title: Characteristics of Living Things
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Identify and describe characteristics of a greater variety of animals and plants and demonstrate an increased ability to categorize them.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Characteristics of Living Things ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Identify and describe characteristics of a greater variety

elements.title: Bodily Processes
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Indicate emerging knowledge of bodily processes (for example, eating, sleeping, breathing, walking) in humans and other animals.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Bodily Processes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Indicate emerging knowledge of bodily processes

elements.title: Bodily Processes
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Indicate greater knowledge of bodily processes (for example, eating, sleeping, breathing, walking) in humans and other animals through more detailed observations and descriptions.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Bodily Processes ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Indicate greater knowledge of bodily processes

elements.title: Living and Nonliving Things
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Expect animate objects (people and animals) to self-initiate movement and to have different insides and biological processes that make them behave differently from inanimate objects.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Living and Nonliving Things ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Expect animate objects (people and animals) to self-initiate movement

elements.title: Living and Nonliving Things
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Indicate knowledge of the difference between living and nonliving things and recognize that only living things (people, animals, plants) undergo biological changes such as growth, illness, healing, and dying.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Living and Nonliving Things ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Indicate knowledge of the difference between living and nonliving things

elements.title: Heredity and Traits
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Name and describe similarities and differences they observe between grown animals, including humans, and their young ones.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Heredity and Traits ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Name and describe similarities and differences

elements.title: Heredity and Traits
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Express their expectation that young animals and plants will reflect similar characteristics to grown animals and plants of the same kind.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Heredity and Traits ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Express their expectation that young animals and plants will reflect similar characteristics

elements.title: Habitats
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Identify the habitats of people and familiar animals and plants and communicate their understanding that living things have different habitats.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Habitats ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Identify the habitats of people and familiar animals and plants

elements.title: Habitats
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize that living things have different habitats suited to their unique needs.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Habitats ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Recognize that living things have different habitats suited to their unique needs

elements.title: Changes in Living Things
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Changes in Living Things

elements.title: Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Observe and explore growth in humans, animals, and plants and communicate an increased understanding that living things change as they grow and age.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Observe and explore growth in humans, animals, and plants

elements.title: Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Observe and explore growth and changes in humans, animals, and plants and communicate an understanding that living things change over time in size and other transformations related to an individual's life cycle (for example, birth, growth, reproduction, death). Describe capacities as they grow and age.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Growth, Change, and the Life Cycle of Living Things ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Observe and explore growth and changes

elements.title: Needs of Living Things
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize that animals and plants require care and show an emerging understanding that feeding and watering support the growth and survival of humans, animals, and plants.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Needs of Living Things ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Recognize that animals and plants require care

elements.title: Needs of Living Things
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Describe the needs of humans, animals, and plants for growth and survival (for example, food, water, sleep, sunshine, shelter).
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Needs of Living Things ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Describe the needs of humans, animals, and plants

elements.title: Earth and Space Science
elements.source_text: Strand: 4.0 — Earth and Space Science

elements.title: Properties and Characteristics of Earth Materials and Objects
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Properties and Characteristics of Earth Materials and Objects

elements.title: Characteristics of Earth Materials
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Investigate and describe the characteristics (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture) of earth materials such as sand, rocks, soil, water, and air.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Characteristics of Earth Materials ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Investigate and describe the characteristics

elements.title: Characteristics of Earth Materials
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Investigate and describe the characteristics of earth materials and compare and contrast materials based on their different features (for example, size, weight, shape, color, texture).
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Characteristics of Earth Materials ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Investigate and describe the characteristics of earth materials and compare and contrast

elements.title: Changes in the Earth and Space
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Changes in the Earth and Space

elements.title: Natural Objects in the Sky
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Observe and describe natural objects in the sky (sun, moon, stars, and clouds).
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Natural Objects in the Sky ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Observe and describe natural objects in the sky

elements.title: Natural Objects in the Sky
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Observe and describe natural objects in the sky and describe patterns of movement and apparent changes in the sun, moon, stars, and clouds.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Natural Objects in the Sky ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Observe and describe natural objects in the sky and describe patterns of movement

elements.title: Weather
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Notice and describe changes in weather. Notice the effects of weather and seasonal changes on their own lives and on plants and animals.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Weather ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Notice and describe changes in weather

elements.title: Weather
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Observe and describe changes in weather and provide examples of the effects of changes in weather and seasons on their own lives and on plants and animals.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Weather ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Observe and describe changes in weather and provide examples

elements.title: Earth and Human Activity
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice, with adult prompting and support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, and participate in activities related to caring for the environment.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Earth and Human Activity ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Notice, with adult prompting and support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment

elements.title: Earth and Human Activity
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Investigate, with adult support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, discuss in simple terms how to care for the environment, and participate in activities related to its care.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Earth and Human Activity ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Investigate, with adult support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment

elements.title: Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science
elements.source_text: Strand: 5.0 — Engineering, Technology, and Applications of Science

elements.title: Engineering Design
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - - Engineering Design

elements.title: Engineering Design Process
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating simple solutions to the problems they identify, and, with adult support, testing and refining their solutions.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Engineering Design Process ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design

elements.title: Engineering Design Process
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating more detailed solutions to the problems they identify, and testing and refining their solutions with less adult support and over longer periods of time.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Engineering Design Process ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems

elements.title: Engineering Design and Society
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Engineering Design and Society

elements.title: Natural Objects in the Sky
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Observe and describe natural objects in the sky (sun, moon, stars, and clouds).
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Natural Objects in the Sky Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Observe and describe natural objects in the sky (sun, moon, stars, and clouds).

elements.title: Natural Objects in the Sky
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Observe and describe natural objects in the sky and describe patterns of movement and apparent changes in the sun, moon, stars, and clouds.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Natural Objects in the Sky Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Observe and describe natural objects in the sky and describe patterns of movement and apparent changes in the sun, moon, stars, and clouds.

elements.title: Weather
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Notice and describe changes in weather. Notice the effects of weather and seasonal changes on their own lives and on plants and animals.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Weather Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Notice and describe changes in weather. Notice the effects of weather and seasonal changes on their own lives and on plants and animals.

elements.title: Weather
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Observe and describe changes in weather and provide examples of the effects of changes in weather and seasons on their own lives and on plants and animals.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Weather Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Observe and describe changes in weather and provide examples of the effects of changes in weather and seasons on their own lives and on plants and animals.

elements.title: Earth and Human Activity
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice, with adult prompting and support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, participate in activities related to caring for the environment.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Earth and Human Activity Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Notice, with adult prompting and support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, participate in activities related to caring for the environment.

elements.title: Earth and Human Activity
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Investigate, with adult support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, discuss in simple terms how to care for the environment, and participate in activities related to its care.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Earth and Human Activity Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Investigate, with adult support, how humans' actions and use of resources impact the environment and their community, discuss in simple terms how to care for the environment, and participate in activities related to its care.

elements.title: Engineering Design Process
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating simple solutions to the problems they identify, and testing and refining their solutions.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Engineering Design Process Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating simple solutions to the problems they identify, and testing and refining their solutions.

elements.title: Engineering Design Process
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating more detailed solutions to the problems they identify, and, with adult support, testing and refining their solutions with less adult support and over longer periods of time.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Engineering Design Process Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Engage collaboratively with peers and adults in engineering design by identifying problems in play and everyday activities, planning and creating more detailed solutions to the problems they identify, and, with adult support, testing and refining their solutions with less adult support and over longer periods of time.

elements.title: Design Solutions and Society
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice and explore, with adult support, how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and goals in everyday life.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Design Solutions and Society Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Notice and explore, with adult support, how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and goals in everyday life.

elements.title: Design Solutions and Society
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Explore in more detail how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and, with adult support, develop different solutions to address the needs of their families and communities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Design Solutions and Society Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Explore in more detail how tools and design solutions help address their own and other people's needs and, with adult support, develop different solutions to address the needs of their families and communities.

elements.title: Using Digital Devices
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate emerging understanding that different digital tools and devices serve different functions (for example, taking videos or photos) and use digital tools, with adult support, to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Using Digital Devices Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Demonstrate emerging understanding that different digital tools and devices serve different functions (for example, taking videos or photos) and use digital tools, with adult support, to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.

elements.title: Using Digital Devices
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize a greater diversity of digital tools and devices and their function (for example, look up information, learn or practice a new skill) and use them with less adult support to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Using Digital Devices Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Recognize a greater diversity of digital tools and devices and their function (for example, look up information, learn or practice a new skill) and use them with less adult support to meet their needs and goals in everyday situations.

elements.title: Physical Development
elements.source_text: Physical Development

elements.title: Fundamental Movement Skills
elements.source_text: Strand: 1.0 — Fundamental Movement Skills

elements.title: Balance
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Balance

elements.title: Balancing While Still
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Show increased balance and control when holding still.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Balancing While Still Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Show increased balance and control when holding still.

elements.title: Balancing While Still
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Maintain balance while holding still; sometimes may need assistance.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Balancing While Still Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Maintain balance while holding still; sometimes may need assistance.

elements.title: Balancing in Motion
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Show increased balance control while moving in different directions and when transitioning from one movement or position to another.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Balancing in Motion Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Show increased balance control while moving in different directions and when transitioning from one movement or position to another.

elements.title: Balancing in Motion
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Maintain balance while in motion when moving from one position to another or when changing directions, though balance may not be completely stable.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Balancing in Motion Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Maintain balance while in motion when moving from one position to another or when changing directions, though balance may not be completely stable.

elements.title: Locomotor Skills
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Locomotor Skills

elements.title: Walking with Balance
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Walk with balance, not always stable. Sometimes swing their arms opposite their legs while walking (for example, step with the right foot, swing their left arm forward).
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Walking with Balance Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Walk with balance, not always stable. Sometimes swing their arms opposite their legs while walking

elements.title: Walking with Balance
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Walk with balance and swing their arms opposite their legs (for example, step with the right foot, swing their left arm forward). Exhibit narrower space between feet while walking.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Walking with Balance Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Walk with balance and swing their arms opposite their legs

elements.title: Running
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Run with a short stride length and feet off the ground for a short period of time. May have difficulty stopping on time. Show inconsistent swinging of the opposite arm and leg together while running.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Running Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Run with a short stride length and feet off the ground for a short period of time.

elements.title: Running
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Run with a longer stride length and each foot off the ground for a longer period of time. Show more control when stopping running. Swing their arm while stepping with the opposite leg more consistently.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Running Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Run with a longer stride length and each foot off the ground for a longer period of time.

elements.title: Jumping
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Jump on two feet for height and distance, including jumping up from the ground or down off a raised surface.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Jumping Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Jump on two feet for height and distance, including jumping up from the ground or down off a raised surface.

elements.title: Jumping
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Jump on two feet for height and distance with increased competence, including jumping up from the ground or down off a raised surface. Swing arms to propel themselves while jumping.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Jumping Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Jump on two feet for height and distance with increased competence

elements.title: Varied Locomotor Skills
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate a variety of new locomotor skills in a basic way that build on (and go beyond) walking and running, such as hopping, galloping, skipping, side-sliding, and leaping.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Varied Locomotor Skills Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Demonstrate a variety of new locomotor skills in a basic way

elements.title: Varied Locomotor Skills
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate increased ability in performing locomotor skills that build on (and go beyond) walking and running, including engagement in hopping, galloping, skipping, side-sliding, and leaping.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Varied Locomotor Skills Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Demonstrate increased ability in performing locomotor skills

elements.title: Manipulative Skills
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Manipulative Skills

elements.title: Gross Motor Manipulative Skills
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Show gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet to interact with objects.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Gross Motor Manipulative Skills Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Show gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet to interact with objects.

elements.title: Gross Motor Manipulative Skills
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show increased ability to perform gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet with increased coordination to interact with objects.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Gross Motor Manipulative Skills Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Show increased ability to perform gross motor manipulative skills

elements.title: Fine Motor Manipulative Skills
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Show some fine motor manipulation skills that involve using hands and fingers to interact with objects used in daily life.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Fine Motor Manipulative Skills Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Show some fine motor manipulation skills

elements.title: Fine Motor Manipulative Skills
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate increased fine motor manipulation skills using hands and fingers with increasing competence and precision to interact with objects needed for daily life.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Fine Motor Manipulative Skills Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Demonstrate increased fine motor manipulation skills

elements.title: Physical Development
elements.description: California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations - Physical Development
elements.source_text: Physical Development

elements.title: Running
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Run with a short stride length and feet off the ground for a short period of time. May have difficulty stopping on time. Show inconsistent swinging of the opposite arm and leg together while running. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Run with a longer stride length and each foot off the ground for a longer period of time. Show more control when stopping running. Swing their arm while stepping with the opposite leg more consistently.
elements.source_text: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Run with a short stride length and feet off the ground for a short period of time.

elements.title: Jumping
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Jump on two feet for height and distance, including jumping up from the ground or down off a raised surface. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Jump on two feet for height and distance with increased competence, including jumping up from the ground or down off a raised surface. Swing arms to propel themselves while jumping.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Jumping

elements.title: Varied Locomotor Skills
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate a variety of new locomotor skills in a basic way that build on (and go beyond) walking and running, such as hopping, galloping, skipping, side-sliding, and leaping. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate increased ability in performing locomotor skills that build on (and go beyond) walking and running, including engagement in hopping, galloping, skipping, side-sliding, and leaping.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Varied Locomotor Skills

elements.title: Manipulative Skills
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Manipulative Skills

elements.title: Gross Motor Manipulative Skills
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Show some gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet to interact with objects. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show increased ability to perform gross motor manipulative skills that involve using arms, hands, and feet with increased coordination to interact with objects.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Gross Motor Manipulative Skills

elements.title: Fine Motor Manipulative Skills
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Show some fine motor manipulation skills that involve using hands and fingers to interact with objects used in daily life. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate increased fine motor manipulation skills using hands and fingers with increasing competence and precision to interact with objects needed for daily life.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Fine Motor Manipulative Skills

elements.title: Hand Preference
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Tend to show a preference for using one hand more than the other within a task and sometimes across multiple tasks. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate a consistent preference for using one hand more often than the other within and across different tasks.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Hand Preference

elements.title: Perceptual-Motor Skills and Movement Concepts
elements.source_text: Strand: 2.0 — Perceptual-Motor Skills and Movement Concepts

elements.title: Body Awareness
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Body Awareness

elements.title: Knowledge of Body Parts
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of the names of basic body parts. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of more and a greater variety of body parts.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Knowledge of Body Parts

elements.title: Spatial Awareness
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Spatial Awareness

elements.title: Spatial Awareness
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use their own body as a reference point when locating or relating to other people or objects in their immediate environment. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use their own body, general space, and other people's space when locating or relating to other people or objects in their immediate environment.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Spatial Awareness

elements.title: Directional Awareness
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Directional Awareness

elements.title: Directional Understanding
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Distinguish the direction of movements that are up and down and to the side of the body. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Understand and distinguish between the sides of the body (without necessarily understanding right and left).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Directional Understanding

elements.title: Directional Movement
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Move forward and backward or up and down easily. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Change directions, moving forward and backward, side to side, or up and down, quickly and with more accuracy.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Directional Movement

elements.title: Object Locations
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an ability to place an object on top of or under something with some accuracy. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an ability to place an object or their own body in front of, to the side of, or behind something else with accuracy.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Object Locations

elements.title: Active Physical Play
elements.source_text: Strand: 3.0 — Active Physical Play

elements.title: Active Participation
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Active Participation

elements.title: Physical Activity
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Initiate or engage in simple physical activities for a short to moderate period of time. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Initiate more complex physical activities for a sustained period of time.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Physical Activity

elements.title: Cardiovascular Endurance
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Cardiovascular Endurance

elements.title: Cardiovascular Endurance
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engage in frequent bursts of active play that involves increased activity of the heart, lungs, and vascular system. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Engage in sustained active play of increasing intensity that involves the heart, lungs, and vascular system.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Cardiovascular Endurance

elements.title: Muscular Strength, Muscular Endurance, and Flexibility
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Muscular Strength, Muscular Endurance, and Flexibility

elements.title: Strength, Endurance, and Flexibility
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engage in active play activities that enhance muscular strength throughout the body, muscular endurance, and flexibility. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Engage in increased active play activities that enhance muscular strength throughout the body, muscular endurance, and flexibility.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Strength, Endurance, and Flexibility

elements.title: Health
elements.source_text: Health

elements.title: Understanding Health and Wellness
elements.source_text: Strand: 1.0 — Understanding Health and Wellness

elements.title: Body and Health Awareness
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Body and Health Awareness

elements.title: Identifying and Naming Body Parts
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Identify and correctly name a few external body parts (for example, elbow, head, private body parts) and internal body parts (for example, bones, brain, heart) and demonstrate limited knowledge of their functions. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify and correctly name several external body parts (for example, elbow, head, private body parts) and a few internal body parts (for example, bones, brain, heart) and demonstrate more detailed knowledge of their functions.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Identifying and Naming Body Parts

elements.title: Communicating About Health Needs
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Communicate to an adult, with varying specificity and accuracy, about feeling uncomfortable, not feeling well, or a special health need. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Consistently communicate to an adult about feeling uncomfortable, not feeling well, or a special health need and can identify a solution.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Communicating About Health Needs

elements.title: Understanding the Role of Health Care Providers
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Communicate their basic understanding that health care providers keep people well and help them when they are not well. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Communicate more specific knowledge on how health care providers keep people well and help them when they are not well.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Understanding the Role of Health Care Providers

elements.title: Body and Safety Boundaries
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Body and Safety Boundaries

elements.title: Recognizing and Communicating About Body Boundaries
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an ability to recognize and communicate about body boundaries, including wanted touches (for example, hug from a peer, high five with a teacher) and unwanted touches (for example, hitting, pushing, inappropriate touches). Tend to follow trusted adults' guidance about body boundaries. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Recognize and communicate, with adult support, about body boundaries, including wanted touches (for example, hug from a peer, high five with a teacher) and unwanted touches (for example, hitting, pushing, inappropriate touches). More consistently and independently follow and use trusted adults' guidance about body boundaries.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Recognizing and Communicating About Body Boundaries

elements.title: Nutrition
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Nutrition

elements.title: Identifying Foods
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Identify a few specific foods. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify a larger variety of foods and know some of the related food groups.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Identifying Foods

elements.title: Communicating Fullness and Hunger
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an emerging ability to communicate their own fullness and hunger to an adult. Anticipate mealtime routines but show a limited capacity to wait for the next meal. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Communicate more consistently their fullness and hunger to an adult. Anticipate mealtime routines and wait a little longer for a meal.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Fullness and Hunger

elements.title: Understanding a Variety of Foods
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an emerging understanding that eating a variety of food helps them grow and feel good. Choose familiar foods, at mealtimes, including familial and cultural foods. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate an understanding that eating a variety of food helps the body grow and feel good. Choose from a greater variety of foods including familial and cultural foods, although occasionally are open to trying new foods.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Understanding a Variety of Foods

elements.title: Physical Activity
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Physical Activity

elements.title: Recognizing the Body's Response to Physical Activity
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize, with adult support, the body's response to physical activity (for example, heart beating fast, sweating, needing water) and demonstrate an emerging understanding that being active is healthy. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Recognize, with less or limited adult support, the body's response to physical activity (for example, heart beating fast, sweating, needing water) and indicate the need to be physically active outdoors or indoors. More consistently and with less adult support indicate the need to be physically active outdoors or indoors.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Recognizing the Body's Response to Physical Activity

elements.title: Sleep
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Sleep

elements.title: Recognizing and Indicating When Tired
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize, with adult support, when they are tired and express tiredness with a range of behaviors. Demonstrate limited ability to follow sleep routines consistently. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Indicate when they are tired with a range of behaviors and demonstrate an emerging ability to understand that sleep and rest are part of keeping their bodies healthy. Follow sleep and rest routines more consistently.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Recognizing and Indicating When Tired

elements.title: Health and Safety Habits
elements.source_text: Strand: 2.0 — Health and Safety Habits

elements.title: Basic Hygiene
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Basic Hygiene

elements.title: Handwashing
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of some steps in the handwashing routine. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of most or all steps in the handwashing routine.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Handwashing

elements.title: Preventing Infectious Diseases
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Practice health habits that prevent infectious diseases and infestations (for example, lice). Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Practice health habits that prevent infectious diseases and infestations (for example, lice).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Preventing Infectious Diseases

elements.title: Recognizing and Indicating When Tired
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize, with adult support, when they are tired and express tiredness with a range of behaviors. Demonstrate limited ability to follow sleep routines consistently. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Indicate when they are tired with a range of behaviors and demonstrate an emerging ability to understand that sleep and rest are part of keeping their bodies healthy. Follow sleep and rest routines more consistently.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Recognizing and Indicating When Tired

elements.title: Health and Safety Habits
elements.source_text: Strand: 2.0 — Health and Safety Habits

elements.title: Handwashing
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of some steps in the handwashing routine. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of most or all steps in the handwashing routine.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Handwashing

elements.title: Preventing Infectious Diseases
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Practice health habits that prevent infectious diseases and infestations (for example, lice) with adult instruction and modeling. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Practice health habits that prevent infectious diseases and infestations (for example, lice) with limited adult instruction and modeling.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Preventing Infectious Diseases

elements.title: Oral Health
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Oral Health

elements.title: Toothbrushing
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge and follow some steps of the toothbrushing routine with adult supervision and instruction. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge and follow more steps of the toothbrushing routine and demonstrate knowledge of when toothbrushing should be done with limited adult supervision and instruction.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Toothbrushing

elements.title: Sun Safety
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Sun Safety

elements.title: Practicing Sun Safety
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Practice some sun-safe actions (for example, wearing sunscreen, drinking water) with adult support and guidance. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Practice sun-safe actions (for example, wearing sunscreen, drinking water) with less adult support and guidance.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Practicing Sun Safety

elements.title: Injury Prevention
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Injury Prevention

elements.title: Following Safety Rules
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Follow indoor and outdoor safety rules (that is, any rules that protect children from danger, risk, or injury) with adult support and prompting. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Follow indoor and outdoor safety rules (that is, any rules that protect children from danger, risk, or injury) with less adult support and guidance.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Following Safety Rules

elements.title: Following Emergency Routines
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an ability to follow emergency routines (for example, fire drill, earthquake drill) after instruction and practice with adult support and guidance. Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate increased independent ability to follow emergency routines (for example, fire drill) after instruction and practice with some adult guidance.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Following Emergency Routines

elements.title: Following Transportation and Pedestrian Safety Rules
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Show an emerging ability to follow and understand transportation and pedestrian safety rules with adult support and supervision (for example, look both ways before crossing the street, help buckle the harness straps in a car seat). Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show increased ability to follow transportation and pedestrian safety rules with adult instruction and supervision (for example, look both ways before crossing the street, help buckle the harness straps in a car seat).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Following Transportation and Pedestrian Safety Rules

elements.title: History-Social Science
elements.source_text: History-Social Science

elements.title: Social Inquiry Skills
elements.source_text: Strand: 1.0 — Social Inquiry Skills

elements.title: Asking Questions and Using Evidence
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Asking Questions and Using Evidence

elements.title: Making Observations and Asking Questions
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice, make observations, and ask adults and peers questions about the social world (people, places, institutions). Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Make more detailed observations and ask adults and peers more specific questions (why, how) about the social world (people, places, institutions).
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations and Asking Questions

elements.title: Gathering and Using Evidence
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Gather information with adult support from resources (such as books and other media) related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions). Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Gather information during extended inquiries, with adult support, from a greater variety of resources (such as informational books, magazines, media, and community members) to generate answers related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions).
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Gathering and Using Evidence

elements.title: Communicating Ideas About the Social World
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Communicating Ideas About the Social World

elements.title: Creating Representations
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Create representations (for example, drawings, three-dimensional models, embodied action, or stories) to show developing understanding of the social world (people, places, institutions), with adult support. Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Create more detailed representations (for example, drawings, three-dimensional models, embodied action, or stories) to deepen and share their understanding of the social world (people, places, institutions), with adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Creating Representations

elements.title: Self and Social Systems
elements.source_text: Strand: 2.0 — Self and Social Systems

elements.title: Self-Identity and Society
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Self-Identity and Society

elements.title: Self-Identity
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Show awareness of characteristics of self, Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show greater awareness and understanding
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Self-Identity

elements.title: Making Observations and Asking Questions
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice, make observations, and ask adults and peers questions about the social world (people, places, institutions).
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations and Asking Questions — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice, make observations, and ask adults and peers questions about the social world (people, places, institutions).

elements.title: Making Observations and Asking Questions
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Make more detailed observations and ask adults and peers more specific questions (why, how) about the social world (people, places, institutions).
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Making Observations and Asking Questions — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Make more detailed observations and ask adults and peers more specific questions (why, how) about the social world (people, places, institutions).

elements.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Gathering and Using Evidence — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Gather information with adult support from resources (such as books and other media) related to questions of interest about the social world (people, places, institutions).

elements.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Creating Representations — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Create more detailed representations...

elements.title: Self-Identity
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Show awareness of characteristics of self, including ethnic, racial, linguistic, religious, gender, and ability identities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Self-Identity — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Show awareness of characteristics of self...

elements.title: Self-Identity
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show greater awareness and understanding of characteristics of self, including ethnic, racial, linguistic, religious, gender, and ability identities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Self-Identity — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show greater awareness and understanding of characteristics of self...

elements.title: Membership in Communities
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of the communities (for example, family, peer group) with whom they interact frequently.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Membership in Communities — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of the communities...

elements.title: Membership in Communities
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of their membership in varied communities, including communities with whom they interact occasionally (for example, sports team, extended family, faith community).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Membership in Communities — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of their membership in varied communities...

elements.title: Awareness of Social Roles
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of familiar everyday social roles (for example, parent, sibling, teacher, doctor).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Awareness of Social Roles — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Exhibit awareness of familiar everyday social roles...

elements.title: Awareness of Social Roles
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Exhibit awareness of broader social roles beyond the everyday social roles they typically encounter.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Awareness of Social Roles — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Exhibit awareness of broader social roles...

elements.title: Culture, Difference, and Diversity
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Culture, Difference, and Diversity

elements.title: Exploring Cultural Communities
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Explore the cultural groups (for example, racial, ethnic, religious, linguistic, ability) in which they participate and display curiosity about other people's practices.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Cultural Communities — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Explore the cultural groups...

elements.title: Exploring Cultural Communities
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Explore characteristics, practices, and traditions of cultural groups (for example, racial, ethnic, religious, linguistic, ability) beyond their own cultural communities, with adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Cultural Communities — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Explore characteristics, practices, and traditions...

elements.title: Exploring Similarities and Differences
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice similarities and differences in people and families they know (for example, race, ethnicity, language, gender expression, family structures, ability).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Similarities and Differences — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice similarities and differences...

elements.title: Exploring Similarities and Differences
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Identify and name similarities and differences among people and families they know (for example, race, ethnicity, language, gender expression, family structures, ability), as well as people they are exposed to through learning materials, media, and daily interactions, with adult prompting.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Similarities and Differences — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Identify and name similarities and differences...

elements.title: Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)
elements.source_text: Strand: 3.0 — Skills for Democracy and Being a Community Member (Civics)

elements.title: Fairness and Respect for Other People
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Fairness and Respect for Other People

elements.title: Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize members of their peer groups (for example, members of their classroom, team, or activity group) and include them in play and learning activities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize members of their peer groups...

elements.title: Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify members of their peer groups (for example, members of their classroom, team, or activity group), and include them in collaborative play and learning activities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Identifying and Including Members of Peer Groups — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify members of their peer groups...

elements.title: Showing Care and Offering Help
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice the needs of other people (for example, adults, peers) and the physical space; show care by offering help and contributing.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Showing Care and Offering Help — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice the needs of other people...

elements.title: Showing Care and Offering Help
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Increasingly notice individual and group needs. Show care for others (for example, adults, peers) and for the physical space through actions like offering help, contributing, advocating, and leading.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Showing Care and Offering Help — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Increasingly notice individual and group needs...

elements.title: Understanding Different Needs and Fairness
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice and communicate that peers have different needs by offering different resources and taking varied actions. (Even so, they may become frustrated by differences in the distribution of materials or opportunities.)
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Different Needs and Fairness — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice and communicate that peers have different needs...

elements.title: Understanding Different Needs and Fairness
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate understanding that peers have different needs by working toward supporting rules and practices that allow for each other's needs to be met. (Even so, they may still become frustrated by differences in the distribution of materials or opportunities.)
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Different Needs and Fairness — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate understanding that peers have different needs...

elements.title: Community Norms and Practices
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Community Norms and Practices

elements.title: Contributing to the Group
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Contribute to group efforts alongside adults and peers.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Contributing to the Group — Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Contribute to group efforts alongside adults and peers.

elements.title: Contributing to the Group
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Contribute ideas, work toward group efforts, and show awareness of their individual contribution to collective group projects alongside adults and peers.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Contributing to the Group — Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Contribute ideas, work toward group efforts...

elements.title: Following Community Rules and Norms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize community rules and norms and, with adult support, follow rules while learning.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Following Community Rules and Norms — Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize community rules and norms and, with adult support, follow rules while learning

elements.title: Following Community Rules and Norms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize rules and norms and follow community rules with fewer adult reminders.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Following Community Rules and Norms — Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize rules and norms and follow community rules with fewer adult reminders.

elements.title: Showing Care and Offering Help
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice the needs of other people (for example, adults, peers) and the physical space; show care by offering help and contributing.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Showing Care and Offering Help ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Notice the needs of other people (for example, adults, peers) and the physical space; show care by offering help and contributing.

elements.title: Showing Care and Offering Help
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Increasingly notice individual and group needs. Show care for others (for example, adults, peers) and for the physical space through actions like offering help, contributing, advocating, and leading.

elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Different Needs and Fairness

elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate understanding that peers have different needs by offering different resources and taking varied actions. (Even so, they may become frustrated by differences in the distribution of materials or opportunities.)
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Understanding Different Needs and Fairness
