elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Contributing to the Group

elements.title: Contributing to the Group
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Contribute ideas, work toward group efforts, and show awareness of their individual contribution to collective group projects alongside adults and peers.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Contributing to the Group

elements.title: Following Community Rules and Norms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize community rules and norms and, with adult support, follow rules while learning to coordinate personal interests with those of others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Following Community Rules and Norms

elements.title: Following Community Rules and Norms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize rules and norms and follow community rules with fewer adult reminders. Enforce community rules and norms with peers.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Following Community Rules and Norms

elements.title: Collaborative Problem-Solving
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Collaborative Problem-Solving

elements.title: Group Decision-Making
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Participate in group decision-making (for example, voting, group meetings, circle time) with adult support. Express ideas of agreement and disagreement during decision-making practices.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Group Decision-Making

elements.title: Group Decision-Making
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Participate in group decision-making (for example, voting, group meetings, circle time) about everyday choices that affect the group, with adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Group Decision-Making

elements.title: Collective Problem-Solving
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Participate in problem-solving with peers (for example, interpersonal conflict resolution). Use simple bargaining or seek out adult intervention as strategies.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Collective Problem-Solving

elements.title: Collective Problem-Solving
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Solve problems with peers through strategies like negotiation and compromise to solve problems. Intervene on behalf of one another during problem-solving.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Collective Problem-Solving

elements.title: Developing Solutions and Taking Action
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Offer simple ideas to address problems and issues affecting their group, school, or larger community, with adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Developing Solutions and Taking Action

elements.title: Developing Solutions and Taking Action
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Develop solutions and take action, often engaging others, to address problems and issues in their group, school, or larger community, with adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Developing Solutions and Taking Action

elements.title: Time, Continuity, and Change
elements.source_text: Strand: 4.0 — Time, Continuity, and Change

elements.title: Understanding of Time
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Understanding of Time

elements.title: Using Time Order Words
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Use sequential words or phrases, such as "before" and "after," to describe the time order of everyday personal events.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Using Time Order Words

elements.title: Using Time Order Words
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Indicate time order with increasing accuracy and sophistication. Use words like "before," "after," "then," "next," "yesterday," and "tomorrow."
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Using Time Order Words

elements.title: Personal History
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Personal History

elements.title: Describing Change Over Time
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Identify change over time in themself, with some adult support, when recalling events from early life (for example, "When I was a baby.").
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Describing Change Over Time

elements.title: Describing Change Over Time
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Identify and describe change over time in themself, family, and community and share more detailed stories about recent experiences.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Describing Change Over Time

elements.title: Historical Changes in People and the World
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Historical Changes in People and the World

elements.title: Recalling Past Events
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recall past experiences easily and enjoy hearing stories about the past but require adult help to distinguish when past events occurred in relation to each other and to connect them with current experience.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Recalling Past Events

elements.title: Recalling Past Events
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show improved ability to relate past events to other past events and current experiences but occasionally rely on some adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Recalling Past Events

elements.title: Sense of Place and Environment
elements.source_text: Strand: 5.0 — Sense of Place and Environment

elements.title: Navigating Familiar Locations
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Navigating Familiar Locations

elements.title: Identifying Characteristics of Locations
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Identify the characteristics of familiar locations, such as home and school and describe objects and activities associated with each.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Identifying Characteristics of Locations

elements.title: Identifying Characteristics of Locations
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Identify characteristics of their community and region, including geographic features, weather, and common activities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Identifying Characteristics of Locations

elements.title: Communicating Locations and Directions
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Communicate location and directionality (for example, in/on, under/over, up/down, inside/outside) when describing nearby places and locations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Communicating Locations and Directions

elements.title: Communicating Locations and Directions
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Communicate a greater diversity of directions to others (for example, near/far, next to, beside/between, in front/behind), including specific places and locations that are farther away.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Communicating Locations and Directions

elements.title: Representations of Physical Space
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Representations of Physical Space

elements.title: Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an emerging understanding that drawings, globes, building materials, and maps can represent the physical world.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps

elements.title: Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Create their own drawings, maps, and models; use globes, maps, and map symbols and use maps for basic problem-solving (for example, locating objects) more skillfully, with adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps

elements.title: Recalling Past Events
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recall past experiences easily and enjoy hearing stories about the past but require adult help to distinguish when past events occurred in relation to each other and to connect them with current experience.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Recalling Past Events ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Recall past experiences easily and enjoy hearing stories about the past but require adult help to distinguish when past events occurred in relation to each other and to connect them with current experience.

elements.title: Recalling Past Events
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show improved ability to relate past events to other past events and current experiences but occasionally rely on some adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Recalling Past Events ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Show improved ability to relate past events to other past events and current experiences but occasionally rely on some adult support.

elements.title: Identifying Characteristics of Locations
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Identify the characteristics of familiar locations, such as home and school and describe objects and activities associated with each.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Identifying Characteristics of Locations ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Identify the characteristics of familiar locations, such as home and school and describe objects and activities associated with each.

elements.title: Identifying Characteristics of Locations
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Identify characteristics of their community and region, including geographic features, weather, and common activities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.1 Identifying Characteristics of Locations ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Identify characteristics of their community and region, including geographic features, weather, and common activities.

elements.title: Communicating Locations and Directions
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Communicate location and directionality (for example, in/on, under/over, up/down, inside/outside) when describing nearby places and locations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Communicating Locations and Directions ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Communicate location and directionality (for example, in/on, under/over, up/down, inside/outside) when describing nearby places and locations.

elements.title: Communicating Locations and Directions
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Communicate a greater diversity of directions to others (for example, near/far, next to, beside/between, in front/behind), including specific places and locations that are farther away.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.2 Communicating Locations and Directions ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Communicate a greater diversity of directions to others (for example, near/far, next to, beside/between, in front/behind), including specific places and locations that are farther away.

elements.title: Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate an emerging understanding that drawings, globes, building materials, and maps can represent the physical world, but often interpret map symbols imprecisely.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Demonstrate an emerging understanding that drawings, globes, building materials, and maps can represent the physical world, but often interpret map symbols imprecisely.

elements.title: Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Create their own drawings, maps, and models; use globes, maps, and map symbols and use maps for basic problem-solving (for example, locating objects) more skillfully, with adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.3 Understanding Physical Space Through Drawings, Building Materials, and Maps ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Create their own drawings, maps, and models; use globes, maps, and map symbols and use maps for basic problem-solving (for example, locating objects) more skillfully, with adult support.

elements.title: Caring for the Natural and Built World
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Caring for the Natural and Built World

elements.title: Caring for the World
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Show an interest in the natural and built world, including animals, plants, and the built environment, especially as they experience it directly. With adult support, develop understanding of the interaction between humans and the environment, including taking care of the natural world (for example, not polluting a lake).
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.4 Caring for the World ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Show an interest in the natural and built world, including animals, plants, and the built environment...

elements.title: Caring for the World
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Show an interest in the natural and built world both within and outside direct experience. Understand the positive and negative impacts of human interaction with the natural world. With adult support, develop actions to take care of the natural world and humans impacted by natural disasters (for example, forest fires).
elements.source_text: Foundation 5.4 Caring for the World ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Show an interest in the natural and built world both within and outside direct experience...

elements.title: Economic Systems
elements.source_text: Strand: 6.0 — Economic Systems

elements.title: Community Needs
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Community Needs

elements.title: Meeting Community Needs
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and that there are different ways to meet those needs.
elements.source_text: Foundation 6.1 Meeting Community Needs ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and that there are different ways to meet those needs.

elements.title: Meeting Community Needs
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and demonstrates emerging ability to identify people and places within the community that help community members meet their needs.
elements.source_text: Foundation 6.1 Meeting Community Needs ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Recognize that community members have basic needs (for example, shelter, food) and demonstrates emerging ability to identify people and places within the community that help community members meet their needs.

elements.title: Awareness of People at Work
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Understand that people do different types of work, both inside and outside the home.
elements.source_text: Foundation 6.2 Awareness of People at Work ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Understand that people do different types of work, both inside and outside the home.

elements.title: Awareness of People at Work
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Explore with adult support a wider array of work that people do both inside and outside the home. Understand how different types of work help communities meet their needs.
elements.source_text: Foundation 6.2 Awareness of People at Work ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Explore with adult support a wider array of work that people do both inside and outside the home. Understand how different types of work help communities meet their needs.

elements.title: Exchange
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Exchange

elements.title: Understanding Exchange
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Recognize use of different forms of exchange, including trading and using money to buy and sell.
elements.source_text: Foundation 6.3 Understanding Exchange ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Recognize use of different forms of exchange, including trading and using money to buy and sell.

elements.title: Understanding Exchange
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Understand more complex exchange concepts, like choosing one item over another (opportunity cost), limited access to or limited amounts of a resource (supply and demand), and the exchange of money to buy and sell goods and services.
elements.source_text: Foundation 6.3 Understanding Exchange ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Understand more complex exchange concepts...

elements.title: Visual and Performing Arts
elements.source_text: Visual and Performing Arts

elements.title: Visual Arts
elements.source_text: Strand: 1.0 — Visual Arts

elements.title: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Visual Art
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Visual Art

elements.title: Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Attend to and engage with different visual art styles and forms (such as paintings, sculptures, and collages), some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Attend to and engage with different visual art styles and forms...

elements.title: Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate increased attention to and engagement with a variety of visual art styles and forms (such as paintings, sculptures, and collages), some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.1 Attending to and Engaging in Visual Arts ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate increased attention to and engagement with a variety of visual art styles and forms...

elements.title: Communicating About Art Forms and Elements
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice and communicate about some objects, forms, or representations that appear in art.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Communicating About Art Forms and Elements ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Notice and communicate about some objects, forms, or representations that appear in art.

elements.title: Communicating About Art Forms and Elements
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Notice and communicate about specific elements that appear in art (such as color, line, texture, or perspective), and describe how objects, forms, or representations are positioned in the artwork.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.2 Communicating About Art Forms and Elements ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Notice and communicate about specific elements that appear in art...

elements.title: Develop Skills in Visual Art
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Develop Skills in Visual Art

elements.title: Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes to create drawings or paintings that suggest people, animals, or other objects.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes to create drawings or paintings that suggest people, animals, or other objects.

elements.title: Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes with increased precision and detail to create drawings or paintings of people, animals, or other objects that are mostly recognizable.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.3 Drawing or Painting Lines and Curves ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Use straight and curved marks and lines, circles, and other shapes with increased precision and detail...

elements.title: Working with Dough or Clay
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Make somewhat regular-shaped balls and coils out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Working with Dough or Clay ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Make somewhat regular-shaped balls and coils out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.

elements.title: Working with Dough or Clay
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Make representational forms that are mostly recognizable out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.4 Working with Dough or Clay ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Make representational forms that are mostly recognizable out of play dough or clay using their hands or simple tools.

elements.title: Using Visual Arts Materials
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures).
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art...

elements.title: Using Visual Arts Materials
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures) that is more detailed.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art...

elements.title: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.

elements.title: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.

elements.title: Demonstrating Motor Control
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.

elements.title: Demonstrating Motor Control
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.

elements.title: Mixing and Blending Colors
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.

elements.title: Mixing and Blending Colors
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.

elements.title: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

elements.title: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that are intended to represent objects, figures, people, or experiences.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that are intended to represent objects, figures, people, or experiences.

elements.title: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that often include a combination of objects, figures, and people to illustrate a story or scene, sometimes naming the artworks.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that often include a combination of objects, figures, and people to illustrate a story or scene, sometimes naming the artworks.

elements.title: Intensity and Mood
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Use some intensity of marks and colors to express a feeling or mood.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Use some intensity of marks and colors to express a feeling or mood.

elements.title: Intensity and Mood
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use intensity of marks and colors more frequently to express a feeling or mood and explain their choice.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Use intensity of marks and colors more frequently to express a feeling or mood and explain their choice.

elements.title: Music
elements.source_text: Strand: 2.0 — Music

elements.title: Using Visual Arts Materials
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures).
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use a range of materials to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures).

elements.title: Using Visual Arts Materials
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art (such as drawings or paintings) and three-dimensional art (such as collages or sculptures) that is more detailed.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.5 Using Visual Arts Materials - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use a range of materials more intentionally to create two-dimensional art...

elements.title: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize and name some materials and tools used for visual arts.

elements.title: Communicating Visual Arts Terms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.6 Communicating Visual Arts Terms - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize and name a greater variety of materials and tools used for visual arts with increased accuracy.

elements.title: Demonstrating Motor Control
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Demonstrate some motor control when working with visual arts tools.

elements.title: Demonstrating Motor Control
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.7 Demonstrating Motor Control - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate coordination and increased motor control when working with visual arts tools.

elements.title: Mixing and Blending Colors
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Experiment with mixing colors and notice different colors and shades.

elements.title: Mixing and Blending Colors
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.8 Mixing and Blending Colors - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Intentionally mix and blend colors to achieve different colors and shades.

elements.title: Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Create, Invent, or Express Through Visual Art

elements.title: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that are intended to represent objects, figures, people, or experiences.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

elements.title: Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Create two-dimensional and three-dimensional pieces of visual art that often include a combination of objects, figures, and people to illustrate a story or scene, sometimes naming the artworks.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.9 Creating Two-Dimensional and Three-Dimensional Representations - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

elements.title: Intensity and Mood
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Use some intensity of marks and colors to express a feeling or mood.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Intensity and Mood
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Use intensity of marks and colors more frequently to express a feeling or mood and explain their choice.
elements.source_text: Foundation 1.10 Intensity and Mood - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Music
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Music

elements.title: Attending to and Engaging in Music
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Attend to and engage with different sounds, vibrations, rhythms, and instruments, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Attending to and Engaging in Music - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Attending to and Engaging in Music
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate increased attention to and engagement with a wider variety of sounds, vibrations, rhythms, and instruments, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.1 Attending to and Engaging in Music - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Responding to Music with Body Movements
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Use body movement to respond to music by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Responding to Music with Body Movements - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Responding to Music with Body Movements
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Use body movement that more closely responds to the beat, dynamics (louds and softs), and tempo (speed) of music by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.2 Responding to Music with Body Movements - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

elements.title: Develop Skills in Music
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Develop Skills in Music

elements.title: Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Notice and communicate differences between some vocal, instrumental, and environmental sounds and vibrations.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

elements.title: Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Notice and communicate differences between a larger array of vocal, instrumental, and environmental sounds and vibrations with increased accuracy.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.3 Recognizing Sounds and Vibrations - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

elements.title: Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Explore vocally and with instruments; sing patterns, chants, and parts of songs by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Expand vocal and instrumental exploration; sing patterns, chants, and entire songs alone or with others in varying pitches (highs and lows), dynamics (louds and softs), moods, and intensities.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.4 Exploring Vocal Expression and Instruments - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Tap beats and rhythms with their hands, feet, and instruments or other percussive tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Increase complexity and accuracy in tapping various beats and rhythms with their hands, feet, and instruments or other percussive tools.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.5 Exploring Beat and Rhythmic Awareness - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Communicating Music Terms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Recognize and name basic terms and elements related to music, such as the names of a few instruments or a few basic terms describing tempo (speed) or dynamics (louds and softs).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Communicating Music Terms - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

elements.title: Communicating Music Terms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Recognize and name a wider range of instruments and specific qualities of music, such as pitch (high or low), mood, tempo (speed), and dynamics (louds and softs).
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.6 Communicating Music Terms - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Create, Invent, or Express Through Music
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Create, Invent, or Express Through Music

elements.title: Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use vocal skills, instruments, and other tools to produce short melodies, chants, or songs using simple rhythms and tones, by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

elements.title: Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Use vocal skills, instruments, and other tools to produce or improvise on melodies, chants, or songs using more complex rhythms and tones, by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 2.7 Producing or Improvising Melodies and Rhythms - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

elements.title: Drama
elements.source_text: Strand: 3.0 — Drama

elements.title: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Drama
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Drama

elements.title: Engaging in Drama
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engage in pretend play by themself or with others, acting out scripts, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Engaging in Drama - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

elements.title: Engaging in Drama
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Engage in extended and flexible pretend play scenarios, by themself or with others, acting out scripts, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.1 Engaging in Drama - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

elements.title: Understanding Plot
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of the simple plot of a participatory drama.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Understanding Plot - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

elements.title: Understanding Plot
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate knowledge of the extended plot and conflict of a participatory drama.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.2 Understanding Plot - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

elements.title: Develop Skills in Drama
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Develop Skills in Drama

elements.title: Showing Emotions
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use gestures, expressions, and movements to intentionally show different emotions.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Showing Emotions - Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)

elements.title: Showing Emotions
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Use gestures, expressions, and movements to intentionally show a wider range of emotions.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.3 Showing Emotions - Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)

elements.title: Acting Out Prompts or Scripts
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Participate in acting out prompts or simple [scripts].
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Acting Out Prompts or Scripts - Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Acting Out Prompts or Scripts
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Participate in acting out longer or more [complex prompts or scripts].
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.4 Acting Out Prompts or Scripts - Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years)

elements.title: Engaging in Role-Play
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a limited range of voices, movements, and gestures.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Engaging in Role-Play ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a limited range of voices, movements, and gestures.

elements.title: Engaging in Role-Play
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a wider range of voices, movements, and gestures.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.5 Engaging in Role-Play ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Engages in role-play (representing a person, animal, or character) using a wider range of voices, movements, and gestures.

elements.title: Vocal Projection
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years): Project their voice with some clarity using some dynamics (louds and softs).
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Vocal Projection ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) ... Project their voice with some clarity using some dynamics (louds and softs).

elements.title: Vocal Projection
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Project their voice with increased clarity using a range of dynamics (louds and softs).
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.6 Vocal Projection ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Project their voice with increased clarity using a range of dynamics (louds and softs).

elements.title: Communicating Drama Terms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to drama.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Communicating Drama Terms ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to drama.

elements.title: Communicating Drama Terms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years): Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to drama and an increased ability to communicate the terms.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.7 Communicating Drama Terms ... Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) ... Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to drama and an increased ability to communicate the terms.

elements.title: Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama

elements.title: Using Props or Costumes
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Add props or costumes to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Using Props or Costumes ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Add props or costumes to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.

elements.title: Using Props or Costumes
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Intentionally use a variety of props, costumes, or scenery to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.8 Using Props or Costumes ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Intentionally use a variety of props, costumes, or scenery to enhance the dramatization of familiar stories and fantasy play by themself or with others.

elements.title: Creating Scripts
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Create new scripts, reinvent, or embellish existing stories and act them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.9 Creating Scripts ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) ... Create new scripts, reinvent, or embellish existing stories and act them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.

elements.title: Creating Scripts
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Intentionally create longer and more detailed scripts and stories, acting them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.
elements.source_text: Foundation 3.9 Creating Scripts ... Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) ... Intentionally create longer and more detailed scripts and stories, acting them out by themself or with others, with or without adult support.

elements.title: Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Create, Invent, or Express Through Drama

elements.title: Dance
elements.source_text: Strand: 4.0 — Dance

elements.title: Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Dance
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Notice, Respond to, or Engage in Dance

elements.title: Attending to and Engaging in Dance
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Attend to or engage in different movements, gestures, and expressions, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Attending to and Engaging in Dance ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Attend to or engage in different movements, gestures, and expressions, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.

elements.title: Attending to and Engaging in Dance
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate increased attention to or engagement with a variety of movements, gestures, and expressions, some of which are familiar or reflect home and community cultural experiences and some of which are new.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.1 Attending to and Engaging in Dance Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Demonstrate increased attention to or engagement with a variety of movements, gestures, and expressions...

elements.title: Develop Skills in Dance
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand - Develop Skills in Dance

elements.title: Spatial Awareness and Coordination
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Demonstrate awareness of self and others in dance or when moving in space.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Spatial Awareness and Coordination ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Demonstrate awareness of self and others in dance or when moving in space.

elements.title: Spatial Awareness and Coordination
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate increased awareness of self and others and coordination of movement in dance or when moving in space.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.2 Spatial Awareness and Coordination Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Demonstrate increased awareness of self and others and coordination of movement in dance or when moving in space.

elements.title: Responding to Tempo
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Responding to Tempo ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement.

elements.title: Responding to Tempo
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement with increased accuracy and skill.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.3 Responding to Tempo Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Respond to tempo (speed) and timing through movement with increased accuracy and skill.

elements.title: Learning Basic Dance Skills
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Respond to the instruction of one skill at a time in movement (such as jumping or falling).
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Learning Basic Dance Skills ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Respond to the instruction of one skill at a time in movement (such as jumping or falling).

elements.title: Learning Basic Dance Skills
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Respond to the instruction of more than one skill at a time in movement (such as turning, leaping, and turning again).
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.4 Learning Basic Dance Skills Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Respond to the instruction of more than one skill at a time in movement (such as turning, leaping, and turning again).

elements.title: Communicating Dance Terms
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to dance.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Communicating Dance Terms ... Early (3 to 4 1/2 Years) Understand and communicate basic terms and elements related to dance.

elements.title: Communicating Dance Terms
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to dance (such as steps, tempo [speed], or types of dances) and an increased ability to communicate the terms.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.5 Communicating Dance Terms Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Demonstrate a broader understanding of terms and elements related to dance (such as steps, tempo [speed], or types of dances) and an increased ability to communicate the terms.

elements.title: Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance
elements.source_text: Sub-Strand — Create, Invent, or Express Through Dance

elements.title: Representation Through Dance
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.6 Representation Through Dance ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize.

elements.title: Representation Through Dance
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize with increased skill.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.6 Representation Through Dance Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Use music and movement patterns to act out and dramatize with increased skill.

elements.title: Inventing and Improvising Dance
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Invent dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.7 Inventing and Improvising Dance ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Invent dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others.

elements.title: Inventing and Improvising Dance
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Invent and recreate dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others. Often initiate a sequence of movements or steps.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.7 Inventing and Improvising Dance Later (4 to 5 ½ Years) Invent and recreate dance movements or improvise dances by themself or with others. Often initiate a sequence of movements or steps.

elements.title: Communicating Feelings Through Dance
elements.description: Early (3 to 4 ½ Years): Communicate simple feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement, by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.8 Communicating Feelings Through Dance ... Early (3 to 4 ½ Years) Communicate simple feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement, by themself or with others.

elements.title: Communicating Feelings Through Dance
elements.description: Later (4 to 5 ½ Years): Communicate more complex feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement by themself or with others.
elements.source_text: Foundation 4.8 Communicating Feelings Through Dance Later (4 to 5 1/2 Years) Communicate more complex feelings spontaneously and intentionally through dance and movement by themself or with others.
